---
title: "About Us"
page-navigation: false
toc: false
page-layout: full
---

:::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-6}

## [Jonathan Wilson](https://jonathanwil.com) {.fw-bold .text-center .mb-2 .fs-5}

![Jonathan Wilson](/images/about/jon.png){fig-alt="Jonathan Wilson" width="28%" fig-align="center"}

::: {.small .lh-sm}
I’m a systems engineer at heart, with roots in construction and over a decade of experience building software across complex domains including DoD satellite systems, cybersecurity analytics, and large-scale data platforms. I hold a degree in Applied Statistics and Computer Science from Brigham Young University and currently focus on designing scalable data architectures and pipelines for modeling, simulation, and real-time analysis of large, interconnected systems. My interests lie at the intersection of machine learning, cyber-physical systems, and digital twins, with a long-term goal of building intelligent, self-operating infrastructure. I am pursuing a master’s in Data Analytics Engineering at George Mason University.
:::

:::

::: {.g-col-6}

## [Karan Nindra](https://www.linkedin.com/in/karan-nindra-347bb8192/) {.fw-bold .text-center .mb-2 .fs-5}

![Karan Nindra](/images/about/karan.jpeg){fig-alt="Karan" width="28%" fig-align="center"}

::: {.small .lh-sm}
I have my bachelor's in Industrial and Systems Engineering, with a minor in statistics, from Virginia Tech and I am currently pursuing a master's in Operations Research at George Mason University. I started out doing logistics engineering for the United States Postal Service and then transitioned into my current role as a systems engineer for the DoD, doing MBSE for both the system of systems and mission level.
:::

:::

::: {.g-col-6}

## [Irena Austin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/irena-austin/) {.fw-bold .text-center .mb-2 .fs-5}

![Irena Austin](/images/about/irena.png){fig-alt="Irena" width="28%" fig-align="center"}

::: {.small .lh-sm}
I am a Human Resources professional in the federal sector currently pursuing a master's in Management and Responsible AI at George Mason University. My work focuses on bridging the gap between traditional HR operations and predictive analytics. In this repository, I apply machine learning models to analyze complex datasets like flight delays and organizational risk.
:::

:::

::: {.g-col-6}

## [Valerie Sokolow](https://www.linkedin.com/in/valerie-sokolow-7278651a4/) {.fw-bold .text-center .mb-2 .fs-5}

![Valerie Sokolow](/images/about/val.png){fig-alt="Val" width="28%" fig-align="center"}

::: {.small .lh-sm}
Val is a member of the OR 568 Machine Learning Project team. Add a short biography here describing technical background, work experience, academic interests, and role on the project team. This can include domain interests, tools, modeling focus, or collaboration strengths.
:::

:::

::::
